reasoning_model_1: The audio generated by Model 1 accurately follows the text script without any errors. The tone is appropriately weary and frustrated, fitting the content of the message. Regarding the specific requirement '带着叹气' (with a sigh) and the dimension 'Non-verbal vocalizations', the model vocalizes the character '唉' (Ai) clearly with a falling tone. While this conveys the semantic meaning of a sigh, it remains a linguistic articulation of the character rather than a distinct non-verbal physiological sound of sighing (a deep audible exhalation). The expression is consistent but lacks the visceral, organic quality of a real non-verbal sigh.
reasoning_model_2: The audio generated by Model 2 follows the text script accurately. The performance regarding the 'Non-verbal vocalizations' is excellent. At the very beginning (0:00-0:01), the model produces a distinct, realistic breathy exhalation—a genuine sigh—before transitioning into the speech. This aligns perfectly with the instruction to include non-verbal features. Throughout the clip, the voice quality maintains a breathy, defeated texture that reinforces the feeling of frustration and exhaustion much more naturally than a standard reading. The prosody is highly realistic and emotive.
model_comparison: Both models successfully produce the correct text and convey a sad, frustrated emotion. However, Model 2 demonstrates superior performance in the specific dimension of 'Non-verbal vocalizations' (非语言发声). Model 1 interprets the instruction largely through the linguistic pronunciation of the character '唉' with a sad tone. In contrast, Model 2 generates an actual physiological sound of a sigh (an audible breath/exhale) at the beginning, distinct from just reading the character. This makes Model 2's output significantly more natural and strictly compliant with the paralinguistic feature request. Model 2's overall prosody also feels more genuinely defeated.
